title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes

input_row.title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
input_row.description: Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses. Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public IP addresses on cluster nodes restricts access to only internal networks, forcing attackers to obtain local network access before attempting to compromise the underlying Kubernetes hosts.
input_row.audit: Check for the following: export OKE_NODEPOOLID= export OKE_NODEID= oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id ${OKE_NODEPOOLID=} --node-id ${OKE_NODEID=} [--query ] [--auth ] [--profile ] [--region ]
input_row.remediation: Default Value: By default, Private Nodes are disabled. Internal Only - General
input_row.impact: To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP range and IP Aliasing enabled. Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can manage your own NAT gateway.
input_row.default_value: By default, Private Nodes are disabled. Internal Only - General
input_row.justification: The task of ensuring clusters are created with private nodes can be automated using AWS APIs. The second AI opinion correctly identifies that in AWS, the equivalent service is Amazon EKS, and the configuration can be verified using the `describe-nodegroup` API call. This allows for checking the `publicAccess` and `privateAccess` settings in the `resourcesVpcConfig` of the node group, which aligns with the compliance requirement of having private nodes.
input_row.automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: Amazon EKS Nodegroups - API/CLI Method: `boto3.client('eks').describe_nodegroup(clusterName='CLUSTER_NAME', nodegroupName='NODEGROUP_NAME')` or `aws eks describe-nodegroup --cluster-name <CLUSTER_NAME> --nodegroup-name <NODEGROUP_NAME>` - Properties to Check: `resourcesVpcConfig.privateAccess` should be `True` and `resourcesVpcConfig.publicAccess` should be `False` - Pass Criteria: `resourcesVpcConfig.privateAccess` is `True` and `resourcesVpcConfig.publicAccess` is `False` - Fail Criteria: `resourcesVpcConfig.privateAccess` is `False` or `resourcesVpcConfig.publicAccess` is `True`

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_oke_node_pool_private_node_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all OKE (Oracle Kubernetes Engine) clusters in the tenancy, per region.
- For each cluster, enumerate all node pools using the OCI CLI command:  
  `oci ce node-pool list --compartment-id <compartment_id> --cluster-id <cluster_id>`
- For each node pool, fetch the node pool details:  
  `oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id <node_pool_id>`
- Examine the node pool configuration, specifically the `nodeConfigDetails` or `nodeMetadata` attributes:
  - Look for settings or flags indicating whether public IP addresses are disabled (i.e., nodes are created with ONLY private IPs).
  - The absence of public IP configuration, or an explicit flag such as `isPublicIpDisabled: true` (exact key might depend on the SDK/CLI version and documentation), indicates compliance.
- **Pass Criteria:** Every node pool in the OKE cluster has public IPs disabled for all nodes (nodes launched only with private IPs).
- **Fail Criteria:** Any node pool allows nodes to have public IP addresses (public IP not disabled).
- To automate, use either:
  - OCI Python SDK: `oci.container_engine.ContainerEngineClient.get_node_pool(node_pool_id)`, parsing relevant attributes (`is_public_ip_enabled` or similar).
  - OCI CLI: `oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id <node_pool_id>` and parse JSON output for public/private IP settings.
- **Note:** This control is entirely checkable via OCI APIs/CLI, as node pool and node configuration are exposed and auditable.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure__OKE__Benchmark_v1_5_0_PDF
